title: Significance of the fans surrounding Kaji and Misato
display_title: Significance of the fans surrounding Kaji and Misato
wikitext:
[]: Significance of the fans surrounding Kaji and Misato?             Quote Post by Protohipster » Tue Mar 05, 2019 9:22 pm Does anyone know the purpose of the cinematographic motif of fans that surround Kaji and Misato? In episode 21, Kaji's death takes place in front of a giant fan SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Significance of the fans surrounding Kaji and Misato?             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Fri Mar 08, 2019 5:22 pm "Air" - maybe??? More seriously, maybe it's the cross designs motif itself??

[]: Re: Significance of the fans surrounding Kaji and Misato?             Quote Post by Protohipster » Tue Mar 19, 2019 9:30 pm SawItAtAge10 wrote: "Air" - maybe??? More seriously, maybe it's the cross designs motif itself??

[]: Re: Significance of the fans surrounding Kaji and Misato?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 20, 2019 8:12 am Just an FYI, guys: This is an in-depth discussion forum for an anime that's over 20 years old. You don't need to spoiler any of NGE's plot details. Eva-01 is in a giant shower being washed down at the end of episode 24. Notice the rain coats.

[]: Re: Significance of the fans surrounding Kaji and Misato?             Quote Post by ACGT-Samael » Sat Mar 23, 2019 7:34 am At the risk of being that guy... is it even necessary that fans have symbolism attached to them now? The fan in Misato's room has a perfectly valid reason to be there: it's perpetual summer and really, really hot. That's why she's often in such light clothing outside of work. It gets tied in with the cross symbolism in End of Evangelion sure, but the original TV version doesn't do anything with the fan other than "it's there". Possibly just as an establishing detail of the room. Sometimes a cigar is just a cigar.

[]: Re: Significance of the fans surrounding Kaji and Misato?             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Mar 26, 2019 12:33 pm The fans represent a watchful eye over these events that are otherwise private, IOW the eyes of the audience (the fans! get it???). In the case of Kaji's death, the audience is the only witness. More significantly, in the case of Misato and Kaji's sex scene, both Shinji and the audience are being exposed to an otherwise intimate and private moment between the two, tying into the themes being explored in that scene which involve Shinji seeing somebody he knows committing sexual acts in private. In this moment Shinji and the fan are one in the same, as the story is addressing both Shinji and the audience at this point. The last example you share is a direct conversation to the audience.

[]: Re: Significance of the fans surrounding Kaji and Misato?             Quote Post by PenPen4life » Sat Mar 30, 2019 6:16 am Specualtion ofc, but i'll give it a shot: Misato depends on Kaji's validation for motivation. When he dies she is unable to do anything and cnnsequently doesn't really do anything when she clearly needed to intervene with Asuka, who in turn Shinji relies on for validation rendering him insecure. When the fan stops spinning is when Misato tells him he doesn't have a reason to be insecure. Misato's telling Shinji how to break the cycle, so the fan stops spinning. At the risk of being that guy... is it even necessary that fans have symbolism attached to them now? The fan in Misato's room has a perfectly valid reason to be there: it's perpetual summer and really, really hot. That's why she's often in such light clothing outside of work. It gets tied in with the cross symbolism in End of Evangelion sure, but the original TV version doesn't do anything with the fan other than "it's there". Possibly just as an establishing detail of the room. Sometimes a cigar is just a cigar.
